perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the party disclosing Confidential Information, as defined below, is the “ Disclosing Party ” and the party receiving the Confidential Information is the “ Recipient ”.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the party disclosing information is the “ Disclosing Party ” and the party receiving the information is the “ Recipient ”.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the word 'Confidential' from the definition of Disclosing and Receiving Party creates an in-text contradiction. Throughout the rest of the agreement, the term 'Confidential Information' is used extensively, implying a specific type of information is being protected. This change broadens the scope to 'information' which includes even non-confidential details. It is inconsistent with the rest of the NDA and contradicts the specific protection intended in the agreement, now that it could apply to any type of information and not specifically confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information will be used solely for the purposes of evaluating the Proposed Transaction, provided, however, that the Recipient may disclose any Confidential Information to its Representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction. Prior to disclosing the Confidential Information to any Representative, however, the Recipient shall inform the Representative of the confidential nature of such information and undertake reasonable efforts to cause them to treat such information on a confidential basis. The Recipient shall be responsible for the breach of this Agreement by its Representatives that have not separately signed a non-disclosure agreement with the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives, and shall take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party and its Representatives shall otherwise have no recourse against any of the Recipient’s Representatives that have not separately signed a non-disclosure agreement with the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The change removes 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information' in the definition of Disclosing and Receiving Party. This broadens the scope of 'information' to include non-confidential details, which contradicts the rest of the agreement, specifically the 'Contradicted Text' which repeatedly references and relies on the concept of 'Confidential Information'. The change creates a clear and direct conflict as the agreement now could be interpreted to protect any type of information and not specifically confidential information, undermining the purpose of the NDA as explained.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information will be used solely for the purposes of evaluating the Proposed Transaction, provided, however, that the Recipient may disclose any Confidential Information to its Representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Information will be used for the purposes of evaluating the Proposed Transaction, provided, however, that the Recipient may disclose the Information to its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information', the clause no longer restricts the use and disclosure to solely confidential information. Now, any information, even that publicly available, falls under this clause, contradicting the fundamental purpose of an NDA. This creates a contradiction because earlier paragraphs defines 'Confidential Information', and using 'Information' here creates uncertainty, making it unclear whether the restrictions meant for confidential material still apply here. This contradicts the purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall at all times remain, as between the parties, the property of the Disclosing Party. No license under any trade secrets, copyrights, or other rights is granted by this Agreement or any disclosure of Confidential Information hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information'. The changed text removes 'Confidential' and refers to 'Information', thereby expanding the scope of the clause to include all information, not just confidential information. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall at all times remain, as between the parties, the property of the Disclosing Party'. By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Information', the changed text contradicts the contradicted text because it is unclear whether the provision related to ownership now extends to all information, including non-confidential information, or just the original confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The public disclosure of the Proposed Transaction could have a material adverse effect on IDT’s and PLX’s businesses if, for any reason, a definitive agreement with respect to such transaction is not consummated.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosure of the Proposed Transaction could have a material adverse effect on IDT’s and PLX’s businesses.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the qualification 'if, for any reason, a definitive agreement with respect to such transaction is not consummated,' the sentence now implies that *any* public disclosure of the Proposed Transaction, regardless of whether an agreement is reached, could have a material adverse effect. This creates an internal contradiction with paragraph 14 which allows termination of discussions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that unless and until a definitive written agreement or other binding agreement between the parties with respect to a relationship of the type contemplated by the Proposed Transaction has been executed and delivered by each party hereto, no party will be under any obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a relationship by virtue of this or any written or oral expression concerning such a transaction by any of its Representatives except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party further acknowledges and agrees that the other party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to make or not to make any proposal and to reject any and all proposals (or requests to make one or more proposals) made by the other party or any of its directors, officers, affiliates, employees, agents, advisors or representatives with regard to the Proposed Transaction, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that disclosure could have a material adverse effect only if a definitive agreement is not consummated. The changed text removes this condition, implying that any disclosure could have a material adverse effect. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows parties to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time, implying no obligation arises until a definitive agreement is in place. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction as it suggests potential harm from disclosure even when no agreement exists, conflicting with the freedom to terminate discussions as outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall at all times remain, as between the parties, the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Information disclosed hereunder shall at all times remain, as between the parties, the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' broadens the scope of ownership. Now, any information disclosed, even if publicly available or not confidential, becomes the property of the disclosing party. This contradicts the general understanding of ownership and intellectual property rights, particularly in the context of evaluating a transaction. It contradicts with paragraph 7, which allows the Recipient to use the information that become publicly available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall prohibit or limit the Recipient’s use of information (including, but not limited to, ideas, concepts, know-how, techniques, and methodologies) (i) that was known to Recipient or any of its Representatives before disclosure by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, (ii) that was or becomes available to the Recipient or any of its Representatives from a source other than the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, provided that such source is not known by the Recipient to be prohibited from disclosing such information by a contractual, legal, or fiduciary obligation to the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, (iii) which is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement or (iv) which is independently developed by Recipient or any of its Representatives without reference to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change broadens the definition of what constitutes proprietary information. Originally, the agreement pertained specifically to 'Confidential Information.' The changed text broadens this to 'Information,' now encompassing all information disclosed, regardless of confidentiality. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which allows the recipient to use information that becomes publicly available. If 'Information' includes publicly available information and all 'Information' is owned by the disclosing party, the recipient's right to use publicly available information, as described in the contradicted text, is nullified. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Recipient shall promptly destroy or return to the Disclosing Party all written Confidential Information (whether prepared by the Disclosing party or its Representatives) and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written materials except that (i) one copy of each electronic version of such materials may be retained by Recipient and its Representatives in their archives pursuant to such party’s standard data backup and disaster recovery plans and (ii) Recipient’s outside legal counsel and accountants may retain one hard copy of such materials in its archives solely for regulatory or corporate records retention policy compliance and for dispute resolution; provided, however, that any Confidential Information contained in such format may not be used for any other purpose and that all obligations contained herein shall, with respect to any Confidential Information so retained, survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s written request, the Recipient shall promptly destroy or return to the Disclosing Party all written Information (whether prepared by the Disclosing party or its Representatives) and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written materials except that (i) one copy of each electronic version of such materials may be retained by Recipient and its Representatives in their archives pursuant to such party’s standard data backup and disaster recovery plans and (ii) Recipient’s outside legal counsel and accountants may retain one hard copy of such materials in its archives solely for regulatory or corporate records retention policy compliance and for dispute resolution; provided, however, that any Information contained in such format may not be used for any other purpose and that all obligations contained herein shall, with respect to any Information so retained, survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' expands the obligation to destroy or return all written material, regardless of its confidentiality. This contradicts the exception clauses (i) and (ii) because the need to retain copies for data backup and regulatory compliance only applies to information that has a long-term value or legal requirement, now it will involve any type of information. It creates contradiction because the exception in this paragraph for the retention of materials now applies to anything disclosed, irrespective of its confidentiality
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall prohibit or limit the Recipient’s use of information (including, but not limited to, ideas, concepts, know-how, techniques, and methodologies) (i) that was known to Recipient or any of its Representatives before disclosure by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, (ii) that was or becomes available to the Recipient or any of its Representatives from a source other than the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, provided that such source is not known by the Recipient to be prohibited from disclosing such information by a contractual, legal, or fiduciary obligation to the Disclosing Party or its Representatives, (iii) which is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement or (iv) which is independently developed by Recipient or any of its Representatives without reference to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' broadens the scope of what must be returned or destroyed upon request. This directly contradicts the clause that allows the Recipient to use information that was already known to them or independently developed (the 'Contradicted Text'). The Changed Text now requires the Recipient to destroy even information they were entitled to use, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency within the contract. The 'Omissions' perturbation type is correct here because essential language ('Confidential') was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished hereby, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the Chief Financial Officer or General Counsel of the other party, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement neither it nor any of its affiliates will, either directly or indirectly, solicit for employment, or otherwise contract for the services of (or cause or seek to cause to leave the employ of other party or any of its affiliates) any person who is now employed or engaged (either as an employee or consultant) or becomes employed or engaged during the term of this Agreement by the other party or its affiliates, other than persons whose employment or engagement shall have been terminated at least six (6) months prior to the date of such solicitation, employment or other contractual arrangements.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Information being furnished hereby, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the Chief Financial Officer or General Counsel of the other party, for a period of twelve (12) months from the date of this Agreement neither it nor any of its affiliates will, either directly or indirectly, solicit for employment, or otherwise contract for the services of (or cause or seek to cause to leave the employ of other party or any of its affiliates) any person who is now employed or engaged (either as an employee or consultant) or becomes employed or engaged during the term of this Agreement by the other party or its affiliates, other than persons whose employment or engagement shall have been terminated at least six (6) months prior to the date of such solicitation, employment or other contractual arrangements.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' in this context implies that the non-solicitation agreement is in consideration of *any* information exchanged, not just confidential information. This creates a contradiction because the non-solicitation clause is typically tied to the sensitive and proprietary nature of confidential information shared during negotiations. The contradiction stems from restricting employee solicitation due to the exchange of information, that is publicly available, which would not warrant such a strict provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to its confidentiality and nondisclosure obligations as set forth in this Agreement, each party’s right to develop, use and market products and services similar to or competitive with those of the other party shall remain unimpaired. Each party acknowledges that the other party may already possess or have developed products or services similar to or competitive with those of the other party disclosed in the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' broadens the scope of the non-solicitation agreement. The contradicted text explicitly states that the right to develop competitive products remains unimpaired, 'subject to its confidentiality and nondisclosure obligations...'. By broadening the scope to all information, the changed text now restricts employee solicitation even for non-confidential information, creating a contradiction with the unimpaired right to develop competitive products using information that is not confidential.
